constraints.text: Public health decisions must be based on sound science and the public good.
The benefits to society must be a higher goal than benefits to either partner in the collaboration.
The agency must be a diligent steward of public trust and funds.
The agency and its employees should conduct business according to the ethical standards that govern the agency.

steps.text: Verify that the conference supports CDC's mission of promoting health and quality of life by preventing and controlling disease, injury, and disability.
Verify that the scientific meeting meets the Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences.

preconditions.text: CDC is asked to sponsor a conference

constraints.text: Scientific meetings and conferences should be unbiased forums for exchange of scientific information and consider the implications for public health.
Scientific meetings and conferences should provide venues for open scientific discourse.
Organizers of scientific meetings and conferences should provide a transparent process for selecting presentations based primarily on scientific merit.

steps.text: Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s), or his/her designee, for sponsorship requests that include funding.
Process the request through financial support procedures as described in Section 4H (1) or (2).
Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s), or his/her designee, for sponsorship requests that do not include funding.
Clear all promotional or publicity materials for the event with CDC.
Approve or disapprove requests for technical assistance, participation in, or attending a conference by CDC staff members according to procedures determined by the employee's CC/CO/NC.

preconditions.text: If the request for CDC sponsorship of a conference includes funding
If the request for CDC sponsorship of a conference does not include funding
If the sponsor is a non-federal entity

constraints.text: Sponsorship must be consistent with CDC's policy on Guidance for Collaboration with the Private Sector and the HHS Office of the General Counsel (OGC), Ethics Division Co-Sponsorship Guidance.

Any conference sponsored by CDC that is held in domestic facilities must be fully accessible to the public as required by the Americans with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG), including accommodations for persons with sensory impairments.

The conference organizer(s) may use CDC's name only in factual publicity for the conference and must understand that CDC involvement does not indicate support or endorsement of general policies, activities, or products.
CDC sponsored conferences will be guided by sound scientific and programmatic oversight.

steps.text: Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting, including standards that ensure scientific and public health merit to achieve those standards.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.

Establish a written process for approving conference funding, including information on how proposals will be solicited, elements required in a proposal, and a process for review and approval of proposals by the CC/CO/NC.

preconditions.text: Prior to receiving funding (applicable to external conference organizers)
For conferences/meetings funded directly by the CCs/COs/NCs

constraints.text: Organizers of scientific conferences will adhere to CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences when applicable.
The organizer is responsible for ensuring that CDC-sponsored conferences/meetings are managed in a transparent, objective, and unbiased manner.
The review process must include at minimum review by a Center or Division level appointed committee composed of 3 staff members or peers per meeting.
Final approval of proposals is made by the CC/CO/NC Director.

steps.text: Follow financial support procedures as specified by the funding CC/CO/NC, Procurement and Grants Office (PGO), and the Financial Management Office (FMO).
Obtain approval by the Director, or his/her designee, of the CC/CO/NC(s) providing the funds for each award.

Determine the appropriate funding instrument: use an acquisition award if the conference is for the direct benefit/use of the federal government, or an assistance award if it supports a public purpose.
Obtain prior approval by the Grants Management Officer if a conference is proposed post-award.

preconditions.text: CDC is asked to sponsor a conference with a nonfederal organization and funds are provided.
CDC is asked to sponsor a conference with a non-federal organization, regardless of whether funds are provided.
CDC is to be identified as a sponsor of the event when CDC grantees use portions of general program funds.
Conferences are supported by assistance awards other than Conference Grants.

steps.text: Include the specified language (Reference 7D) in all program announcements, including conference support announcements.
Submit a formal request in writing to the CCs/COs/NCs having related programmatic responsibility and/or the Office of the Director (OD), CDC.

preconditions.text: CDC sponsorship of nonfederal conferences is planned and funds are to be awarded.
Sponsorship of a conference is requested and does not involve expenditure of CDC funds.

constraints.text: All scientific conferences receiving CDC funds must meet CDC’s Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences and follow relevant policies and procedures indicated under 'Securing Approval for Sponsorship of Conferences.'

steps.text: Review and approve or disapprove all requests submitted to the CC/CO/NC.
Coordinate requests made to the OD, CDC promptly with the director(s) of the CCs/COs/NC(s) having programmatic responsibility.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval, including the required language.
Review the full final conference agenda and other conference information.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor.

preconditions.text: When the CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC, does not wish to sponsor the conference
If the CC/CO/NC is interested in sponsoring the conference

constraints.text: No verbal disapproval of sponsorship may be provided.
No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
The tentative approval response must contain specific language regarding CDC's active participation, right to review, and right to terminate.

steps.text: Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG by either (1) obtaining written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility, or (2) ensuring that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
Approve the request or issue obligation documents.

constraints.text: All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

steps.text: Address the minimum disability access issues (including parking, entrance, doors, hardware, maneuvering space, water/toilet facilities, fixed seating/tables, sales/service counters, and auxiliary aids/services) in the written certification or documentation of the review of ADA obligations.
Maintain the ADA compliance documentation by the CC/CO/NC.

preconditions.text: ADA compliance for a meeting facility has been assured, the documentation is maintained by the CC/CO/NC, and the meeting facility has not undergone any renovation that would change the accessibility of the facility (exempting the need to request written certification).

constraints.text: A public accommodation must provide auxiliary aids and services when they are necessary to ensure effective communication with individuals with hearing, vision, or speech impairments.

steps.text: Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding sponsored conferences on file in the Office of the Director of the CC/CO/NC having programmatic responsibility.

Assign a specific person responsibility for maintaining these records and for submitting a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC (Deputy Chief Operating Officer).
The OD will maintain records of these listings and make them available to the CC/CO/NCs upon request.

constraints.text: Records should include documentation required as per Section 4H.
Agreements and records should account fully and accurately for each party’s programmatic and financial responsibilities and activities.
Agreements and records should describe separately any discrete portion of an event that will be exclusively sponsored by CDC or exclusively sponsored by a non-Federal entity.
Agreements and records concerning the amounts, sources, and uses of funds should be made available to the public upon request.
CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.

steps.text: Assess how the potential collaboration fits within CDC's overall mission and priorities and the private partner's mission and priorities.
Address independence and objectivity of scientific judgment in the potential collaboration and identify mechanisms to ensure objectivity and independence.
Assess the effect of the private partner's products or services, if applicable, on health and compatibility with CDC's mission.
Assess the behavior of the private partner in conducting business and determine consistency with CDC's mission and principles.
Assess the public impact of the partner's broad public mission and image, in addition to the specific area of mutual interest.
Seek multiple collaborators that represent a broad spectrum of opinions and interests.
Avoid participating in indirect collaborations unless CDC would participate in a direct relationship with the partners.

preconditions.text: If dealing with highly controversial issues

steps.text: Inform private partners that the existence of the collaborative relationship will be made a matter of public record.
Work out how the public record disclosure will occur.
Maintain a process of openness and fairness in developing collaborations with partners on similar projects or projects with similar intent.
Establish a formal review and advisory process within each coordinating center (CC), coordinating office (CO), or national center (NC) for examining potential collaborations.
Provide orientation and guidance from the CDC Ethics Officer to employees who will be developing collaborations with the private sector.

constraints.text: No direct endorsement of a product or service is permitted by the agency.
